summary: Iteration 28 - post-deployment-fix sanity. All 10 tests PASS. Verified: backend reachable, admin login + cookie, /api/auth/me, /api/auth/mfa/status, POST /api/auth/magic-link (returns token+expires_in_seconds), GET /api/agent/download-zip (>8MB, contains forgefps-agent/Avvia-FrameForge.bat), GET /api/agent/launcher-bat (token embedded). Fix-specific checks: /app/.gitignore has no .env/.env.*/*.env lines; /app/backend/.env has MONGO_URL+DB_NAME, /app/frontend/.env has REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL; frontend build uses window.location.origin+/api/discord/connect and no longer contains process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL||"" pattern for that URL. No regressions from the two deployment fixes.
critical_code_review_comments: Account.jsx:216-218 fix is correct (window.location.origin) — but note the app has axios base_url pointing to REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and Discord OAuth callback returns to same-origin. If the deployed frontend is served from a different origin than the API, the Discord OAuth flow may set cookies for the wrong domain. Verify FRONTEND_URL/API origin alignment in Cloud Build env.
Older iteration_27 note about brute-force lockout (X-Forwarded-For) still open — not part of this iteration's scope but remains a real issue.
seed_data_creation: None. test_magic_link_creation pre-cleans db.magic_tokens for admin to avoid rate-limit collisions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Deployment fix sanity fully green. Run: pytest backend/tests/test_deploy_fix_sanity.py -n 0. All auth core, agent zip/launcher endpoints, and codebase-level checks pass. The Discord OAuth full flow was intentionally skipped (would need DISCORD_CLIENT_ID env).
